ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, breast, with skin, baked
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for baked chicken breast with skin as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared poultry cuts is limited.

serving.description: 1 medium chicken breast half with skin
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one medium chicken breast half with skin, approximately 130 g, as commonly consumed at a main meal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, with skin, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05013)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo reports 188 kcal, 27.7 g protein, 8.5 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g for baked chicken breast with skin. USDA SR Legacy NDB 05013 (roasted chicken breast with skin) reports 197 kcal, 29.8 g protein, 7.8 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate — closely consistent. HPB values are used as primary per-100g figures given the Singapore provenance of the input record; USDA SR Legacy is used to fill secondary nutrient fields (saturated fat, cholesterol, potassium, vitamins, minerals) not reported by HPB.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Baked chicken breast with skin contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable for foods with negligible carbohydrate content. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to signal that GI measurement is meaningless for this food. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (48 mg/100 g) and moderate potassium (~290 mg/100 g) support a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Baked chicken breast with skin is suitable for hypertension management given its very low sodium content (48 mg/100 g); however, patients should avoid salt-heavy marinades or seasoning blends and may consider removing the skin to further reduce saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content with high protein (27.7 g/100 g) produce no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Baked chicken breast with skin is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains no carbohydrate or sugar and provides 27.7 g protein per 100 g, which supports satiety and glycaemic stability; removing the skin is advisable for long-term management to reduce caloric density and saturated fat.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from skin (~2.4 g/100 g) and cholesterol (~84 mg/100 g) are moderate; lean protein profile is otherwise favourable
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Baked chicken breast with skin provides a lean protein source suitable for cardiovascular health in moderate portions; patients with established cardiovascular disease should remove the skin before eating to minimise saturated fat and cholesterol intake, and should avoid added salt or high-fat sauces during preparation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No positive anti-inflammatory factors present; skin adds saturated fat without anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Baked chicken breast with skin is not recommended as a primary protein choice for rheumatoid arthritis management because it lacks omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, polyphenols, and meaningful dietary fibre; removing the skin and pairing with anti-inflammatory foods such as oily fish, turmeric, or ginger is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked chicken breast with skin is a minimally processed poultry dish consisting of a chicken breast portion cooked with the skin intact in a dry-heat oven environment, typically at 180–200°C until the internal temperature reaches 74°C. The skin renders during baking, contributing a lightly crisp, golden exterior and a moist, tender interior. The flavour is mild, savoury, and slightly richer than skinless preparations due to the fat rendered from the skin. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein food providing 27.7 g protein per 100 g, with moderate fat at 8.5 g (of which approximately 2.4 g is saturated), zero carbohydrate, and very low sodium at 48 mg per 100 g. It is energy-dense at 188 kcal per 100 g. The absence of carbohydrate makes it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. The skin contributes additional saturated fat and cholesterol compared with skinless preparations, which is a consideration for cardiovascular and weight-management contexts. It is widely consumed across Western, Asian, and global cuisines as a main-course protein. Common preparation accompaniments include herbs, lemon, garlic, and olive oil. It is halal when sourced from halal-certified poultry.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (confidence 0.95 per input record). Secondary nutrient values cross-referenced and supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05013 (roasted chicken breast with skin), which is an approved primary source and closely consistent with HPB values. GI is not applicable due to zero carbohydrate content.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as chicken breast with skin is a pure protein-and-fat food containing no starch, sugar, or dietary fibre from the meat itself; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy for roasted chicken breast with skin). Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy). Monounsaturated_fat_g (3.3 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.8 g), cholesterol_mg (84 mg), potassium_mg (290 mg), and all vitamin and mineral values not reported by HPB are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05013 (roasted chicken breast with skin) as the closest approved-source equivalent; values are proportionally consistent with the HPB fat total of 8.5 g/100 g. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value with gi_category 'not_applicable' to satisfy the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric, while clearly signalling that GI measurement is not meaningful for this negligible-carbohydrate food. Input GI value of 0 with confidence 0.339 from 'Embedded GI Database' has been superseded; the gi_source now documents the rationale referencing the University of Sydney GI Database framework. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are defensible best-match assignments; SNOMED CT is not assigned as no confirmed code exists for this specific prepared food item.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name 'Baked Chicken Breast with Skin' is precise and unambiguous. This is a generic Western/global food item with no mandatory non-English local-language requirement under G6 multilingual rules.

Primary source is HPB Singapore Foodo (confidence 0.95). Zero carbohydrate is justified as naturally zero for a pure animal protein-fat food. nutrient_source fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (48.0), saturated_fat_g (2.4), sugar_g (0.0) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and supported by HPB and USDA SR Legacy cross-reference.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is set to numeric 0 to satisfy the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to clearly signal that GI measurement is not meaningful for this zero-carbohydrate food. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. gi_source documents the rationale. The numeric sentinel value 0 combined with gi_category 'not_applicable' is the correct representation for a negligible-carbohydrate food when the validator requires a numeric field.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided as defensible best match for chicken breast with skin. FoodEx2 code A00LC provided as defensible match for baked chicken breast with skin. SNOMED CT is null with documented gap note. No codes are invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains preparation method, taste/texture, nutritional profile, clinical relevance, dietary suitability, and cultural context. common_user_queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms are populated.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly. Additional condition rheumatoid_arthritis included from input.

All strings in aliases_by_language.english appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. No mandatory non-English aliases required as this is a generic Western food item.
